borrowed: Treat WCAG 2.2 as the accessibility baseline for the healthcare service.
Keep accessibility responsibilities explicit in design, development, content, and testing notes.
rejected: Generic public-service page layouts were not copied because the studio is a dense internal governance workbench.

borrowed: Critical warnings include visible text and a semantic warning name rather than relying on color.
Dangerous or consequential actions are described with consequence and next safe action.
rejected: The exact GOV.UK warning component markup was not copied; the local governance shell component system is used instead.

borrowed: Use table semantics, captions, column headers, and row headers for compare-and-scan data.
Keep the matrix table-first rather than turning it into a decorative card grid.
rejected: Numeric table formatting guidance was not relevant to role-capability states.

references.source: WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide grid pattern
borrowed: Avoid ARIA grid unless implementing full composite-widget focus management.
Use native table semantics with focusable controls in cells for this implementation.
rejected: Spreadsheet-like arrow-key grid behavior was rejected because the matrix actions are inspect-and-select, not editable cells.

borrowed: Keyboard access, focus visible, focus order, reflow, non-text contrast, labels, and status-message coverage are explicit test targets.
State meaning is expressed through text and shape in addition to color.
rejected: No conformance claim is made from automated checks alone.

borrowed: Use browser automation to test labels, visible text, keyboard flow, and common accessibility regressions.
Record accessibility evidence as part of the browser suite, while noting that automated checks do not replace manual assessment.
rejected: Axe dependency installation was not added because the repo's existing Playwright suites use custom runners and accessibility snapshots.

borrowed: Capture ARIA/accessibility snapshots for complex UI landmarks and use targeted assertions for core state.
rejected: Large whole-page snapshot gating was rejected to avoid fragile unrelated diffs.

references.source: Government Analysis Function data visualisation guidance
borrowed: The state matrix has text and table parity and does not depend on color alone.
Freeze cards use concise timeline text rather than decorative charts.
rejected: No chart was introduced because role and access authority are better represented as a structured table.

localAlgorithmOverride: External references sharpened accessibility, table, warning, and Playwright verification choices only. They do not override the Phase 0/Phase 9 acting-scope, release-freeze, settlement, masking, or telemetry-fence algorithm.
